tossups.question: While at one of these places, a character claims “they probably have sweaters” while discussing a boat. The philosophy of life is discussed by Emil and George as they watch the title animals at one of these places. While at one of these places, a character describes an affair with a Greek boy when he was “h-o-m-o-s-e-x-u-a-l” for eleven days. David Mamet’s The (*) Duck Variations is set in one of these places, where a character in another play claims God is “A COLOURED QUEEN WHO WEARS A KIMONO” after describing how he used a poisoned hamburger to kill a dog. The question “could I have planned all this?” is asked by Jerry while impaling himself with Peter’s knife at one of these places. For 10 points, Edward Albee’s The Zoo Story is set in what type of location?
tossups.question_sanitized: While at one of these places, a character claims "they probably have sweaters" while discussing a boat. The philosophy of life is discussed by Emil and George as they watch the title animals at one of these places. While at one of these places, a character describes an affair with a Greek boy when he was "h-o-m-o-s-e-x-u-a-l" for eleven days. David Mamet's The (*) Duck Variations is set in one of these places, where a character in another play claims God is "A COLOURED QUEEN WHO WEARS A KIMONO" after describing how he used a poisoned hamburger to kill a dog. The question "could I have planned all this?" is asked by Jerry while impaling himself with Peter's knife at one of these places. For 10 points, Edward Albee's The Zoo Story is set in what type of location?
tossups.answer_sanitized: park benches [accept parks; accept Central Park; reject "zoos"]

tossups.question: Jacques Derrida’s deconstructionism inspired one of these places designed at the site of former Parisian slaughterhouses by Bernard Tschumi (“CHOO-mee”). The trencadís (“trun-ka-THESE”), or “chopped,” method of mosaic was used in one of these places designed by Josep Maria Jujol (“zhoo-ZEP Maria zhoo-ZHOLE”) for a namesake count. A Hypostyle Room with 86 Doric columns is behind a sculpture of a salamander in one of these places overlooking (*) Barcelona that was commissioned by Eusebi Güell (“oh-ZEH-bee GWAIL”). Calvert Vaux (“vox”) helped design one of these places that is home to Bethesda Fountain. An observatory is found in one of these places named Griffith in Los Angeles. For 10 points, Frederick Law Olmsted designed a “Central” example of what sort of public facility in New York City?
tossups.answer: parks [accept Parc de la Villette, Parc Güell, Griffith Park, or Central Park]
tossups.answer_sanitized: parks [accept Parc de la Villette, Parc Guell, Griffith Park, or Central Park]
tossups.question_sanitized: Jacques Derrida's deconstructionism inspired one of these places designed at the site of former Parisian slaughterhouses by Bernard Tschumi ("CHOO-mee"). The trencadis ("trun-ka-THESE"), or "chopped," method of mosaic was used in one of these places designed by Josep Maria Jujol ("zhoo-ZEP Maria zhoo-ZHOLE") for a namesake count. A Hypostyle Room with 86 Doric columns is behind a sculpture of a salamander in one of these places overlooking (*) Barcelona that was commissioned by Eusebi Guell ("oh-ZEH-bee GWAIL"). Calvert Vaux ("vox") helped design one of these places that is home to Bethesda Fountain. An observatory is found in one of these places named Griffith in Los Angeles. For 10 points, Frederick Law Olmsted designed a "Central" example of what sort of public facility in New York City?

tossups.question: Press coverage of a crime committed in this location was analyzed in Joan Didion’s essay “Sentimental Journeys.” People were suspected of committing a crime in this location after police misunderstood their references to the Tone Loc ("tone" loak) song “Wild Thing.” A full-page ad with the message “bring back the death penalty; bring back our police!” was prompted by a crime in this location that resulted in Raymond Kelly convening the Armstrong Commission after Robert Morgenthau vacated the convictions of the men once thought to be responsible. A 2012 Ken Burns documentary examines a crime that occurred in this location during Ed Koch’s (KOTCH’s) term as mayor. In 1989, five men of color were falsely convicted of raping a white jogger in, for 10 points, what public space in Manhattan?
tossups.answer: Central Park [prompt on “New York City” or “NYC” or “Manhattan”]
